title: Apium serbicum
taxonomy: Apium serbicum is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a member of the genus Apium, which consists of several species of aquatic plants. The genus Apium is further classified under the order Apiales, and the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants. The order Apiales is part of the subclass Rosidae, which includes plants with flowers that have a specific arrangement of sepals and petals. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Apium serbicum, also known as Balkan celery, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), characterized by its edible leaves and stalks. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising remedy for various ailments, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Apium serbicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Balkan celery include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
